review: I've had a mix of experiences at this place over the years – some good and some not so great. The service used to be stellar back in my day when it was owned by a different family; they really knew how to make you feel welcome with their personal touch. But times have changed, I suppose. Lately, it feels like everything's just been rushed and impersonal, especially on weekends when the place is packed. The food still seems decent enough – nothing that stands out as exceptional but also not bad at all - fair for a quick bite or casual diner experience if you ask me. I do appreciate their fresh catch-of-the-day specials, though they're priced reasonably and worth checking in on when the weekend crowd thins.
